title: Real-World Mathematics Domain Expansion Round 2
subtitle: Finance, Information Theory, Quantum, Thermodynamics, Chemistry, Biology, Economics

sessions_detail.title: Black-Scholes and Greeks
sessions_detail.eml_boundary: N(d) not EML-exact (erf has infinite complex zeros); approximated as sigmoid(1.702*d) at 9n, max error < 0.009
sessions_detail.key_insight: 75% cost saving from subexpression sharing across the full Greeks DAG (329n standalone → 83n shared)

sessions_detail.title: Portfolio Theory and Risk Metrics
sessions_detail.key_insight: Log return (3n) cross-domain with pharmacokinetics; conditional min in Sortino denominator is outside EML

sessions_detail.title: Information Theory
sessions_detail.key_insight: The sign-definiteness of summands is the single architectural question that determines cost: add_pos(3n) vs add_gen(11n). Shannon uses add_pos (neg-first restructuring), KL forces add_gen — explains 2x cost ratio.

sessions_detail.title: Quantum Mechanics Distributions and Energy Levels
sessions_detail.key_insight: Fermi-Dirac = sigmoid (9n): physics of quantum exclusion and ML classification share identical node structure. Bose-Einstein costs 1n less (8n) — sub(exp,1) vs add_pos(1,exp).

sessions_detail.title: Thermodynamics
sessions_detail.key_insight: Boltzmann entropy (3n) = Shannon entropy structure (constant·ln). Carnot (4n) = Greenshields traffic = Fick diffusion. Stefan-Boltzmann (9n) = Clausius-Clapeyron — both dominated by a single pow node.

sessions_detail.title: Electrochemistry and Equilibrium
sessions_detail.key_insight: Nernst (11n) vs dG form (16n): same physics, 5n gap purely from mathematical form. sub(2n) vs add_gen(11n). Form choice matters as much as physics.

sessions_detail.title: Population Dynamics and Epidemiology
sessions_detail.key_insight: R0 (2n) and herd immunity (3n) are the most policy-critical metrics in epidemiology and cost 10x less than full SIR simulation (20n). The 2n-20n range is the widest of any domain studied.

sessions_detail.title: Economics and Utility Functions
sessions_detail.key_insight: Log utility is a 1n EML primitive — cheapest nonlinear utility. CRRA→log limit drops cost 7n discontinuously. Cobb-Douglas (10n) = chemical rate law (10n): cleanest cross-science identity in catalog.

cross_domain_isomorphisms_new.note: DEML(k*t,1) or mul+DEML pattern; allometric uses pow not DEML
add_pos(3n) + div(2n) + mul(2n) = 7n for saturation; others share arithmetic structure
neg-first restructuring converts to add_pos summands
Stefan-Boltzmann/Clausius dominated by pow; Fermi-Dirac dominated by add_pos+exp+recip chain
Two pow(3n) + two mul(2n) + optional constant — exact structural isomorphism

members: Statistical mechanics partition function Z = sum exp(-E_i/kT)
cross_domain_isomorphisms_new.note: All are sum_i exp(x_i); the central computation of statistical mechanics and attention

eml_boundary_confirmations: Normal CDF N(d): erf has infinite complex zeros → not EML-exact; approximated sigmoid(1.702d)
VaR erfinv: erf inverse → outside EML (infinite zeros by symmetry)
Sortino conditional min: min() is discontinuous → outside EML
Heisenberg delta_x*delta_p (as minimum): min() outside EML; inequality check form (2n) is EML

notable_extremes.cheapest_nonlinear: log utility U=ln(C) at 1n — ln is an EML primitive
notable_extremes.cheapest_quantum: Heisenberg check 2n; de Broglie 2n given p
notable_extremes.most_expensive_standalone: SIR model per-step at 20n; Black-Scholes full 9-output DAG standalone at 329n
notable_extremes.best_form_gap: Nernst 11n vs dG=nFE 16n — same physics, 5n difference from form choice alone

catalog_counts_by_domain.note: Some equations appear in multiple domains (Beer-Lambert, Michaelis-Menten, partition function); catalog counts unique equations

corrections_from_prompts: Logistic growth example value 993→599.9 (corrected r parameter)
Kleiber coefficient 70 vs 73.3 (source variation; 5n cost is invariant)
